rulings.Case Title: LUXIM CORPORATION, A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. JONES DAY, AN OHIO PARTNERSHIP
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Motion For Preliminary Injunction Against Jones Day
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, DECEMBER 18, 2007, LINE 17. PLAINTIFF LUXIM CORPORATION'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction Against Jones Day: The motion for a preliminary injunction is denied. The focus of the alleged misuse of confidential information, based on a presumption as contrasted with fact, is centered in proceedings before the federal Patent and Trademark Office ("PTO") and in litigation in England. The court does not have the authority to determine who may practice before the PTO or the application of the rules of practice before the PTO. The same would appear to be true as to the litigation in England although the parties have not addressed that question. Given the facts presented, it is the Court's conclusion that as a matter of comity it is not appropriate for the Court to grant the relief requested. In so ruling, the Court relies on the decision in In Re Complex Litigation (1991) 232 Cal.App.3d 572, 600-601 and CCP § 1061. Luxim Corporation has filed certain documents provisionally under seal. It appears that those documents are confidential attorney-client communications and/or are protected by the work product doctrine. As a consequence, they are protected under California Rules of Court 2.550 and 2.551 and may be filed under seal. No other portion of the record need be sealed. Luxim is directed to prepare a form of order that conforms to Rule 2.551(e). =(302/PJM/PB)
